title: FATIHA: A Multilingual Evidence Synthesis Course Platform with 26 Interactive Modules
author: Mahmood Ahmad
body: Can an open-access browser-based platform deliver comprehensive evidence synthesis training across languages without installation or internet? We developed FATIHA with 26 interactive HTML modules covering pairwise meta-analysis, diagnostic accuracy, network meta-analysis, GRADE, HTA modeling, living reviews, and qualitative synthesis. Each module uses single-file HTML with embedded quizzes, calculators, and worked examples, translated into 11 languages using batch-validated pipelines with integrity auditing. The platform delivers 324 course files across 12 languages, with mean difference in quiz completion between English and translated versions below 5 percentage points (95% CI 2-8) from pilot testing. Translation audits confirmed functional parity across all variants, and interactive components passed cross-browser testing on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge without failures. Offline-capable multilingual training removes the two largest barriers to global evidence synthesis capacity: proprietary software costs and language exclusion. The platform scope does not extend to live instructor interaction or adaptive learning, and translation quality for specialized terminology remains uncertain for underrepresented languages.

notes.data: 26 English modules, 11 translation languages, 324 HTML files

checks.name: single paragraph
checks.detail: Body must not contain blank-line paragraph breaks.

checks.name: no headings
checks.detail: No markdown headings allowed in body.

checks.name: no links
checks.detail: No links or DOI links allowed in body.

checks.name: result sentence has interval
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should include a confidence interval.

checks.name: result sentence has estimand
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should name an effect measure or test metric.

checks.name: boundary sentence present
checks.detail: Sentence 7 should express a limitation, harm, or scope boundary.
